_comment: Fictional example (ZENW, a made-up high-growth chip designer) illustrating the legacy v1 shape: steep growth fade, margin compression from peak, rising asset intensity, calibrated terminal RNOA/CoE. Never copy real coverage research into templates.

Bull.narrative: The accelerator demand cycle extends 5+ years and ZENW's toolchain lock-in holds; design wins convert to multi-year platform deals. BUT: as the market matures, second-source competition gradually compresses margins and requires more capital investment.

Base.narrative: Demand growth continues but decelerates as the big buyers optimize; second sources take share but ZENW retains platform leadership. Capital intensity rises as the company funds manufacturing partnerships and R&D infrastructure.

Bear.narrative: The capex cycle peaks early; customers' in-house silicon adoption accelerates and export restrictions tighten. Commoditization erodes the toolchain advantage; ZENW must invest heavily to stay relevant, eroding capital efficiency.
